analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet sarcastically questions whether the 'DIRTY' US could build a 'CLEAN network,' implying the US is morally corrupt or untrustworthy. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) — Yes. Labeling the US as 'DIRTY' casts the West/US as immoral, aligning with CH1’s framing of the West as unethical/hostile. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) — No explicit positive portrayal of China or its cooperative role. CH3 (China has an epic history) — Not referenced. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) — Not addressed; no discussion of China’s domestic system or values. CH5 (The government of the Chinese Communist Party succeeds) — Not addressed; no claims about China’s achievements. CH6 (China is interesting culturally/naturally) — Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH1 only.
